title: The Lost Child – Concept Mastery Helper
author: Mulk Raj Anand

concepts.lesson: The child in the story is curious about everything around him. He is attracted to toys, insects, flowers, and different sights on the way to the fair.
concepts.real_example: Children often stop and explore things they find interesting when walking with their parents.

concepts.lesson: The fair is full of colourful and exciting attractions such as sweets, balloons, flowers, snake-charmers, and the roundabout.
concepts.real_example: Festivals and fairs usually have many attractions that excite children.

concepts.lesson: The child wants many things at the fair like burfi, balloons, a garland, and a ride on the roundabout. These desires reflect the innocent wishes of children.
concepts.real_example: Children often want toys, sweets, or rides when visiting amusement parks or fairs.

concepts.lesson: When the child loses his parents, he realizes how important they are to him. He stops caring about toys or sweets and only wants his parents.
concepts.real_example: Children feel safe and secure when they are with their parents.

concepts.lesson: After realizing he is lost, the child becomes frightened and panics. He cries loudly and searches desperately for his parents.
concepts.real_example: Anyone who gets separated from family in a crowded place may feel scared and helpless.

concepts.lesson: A kind stranger tries to comfort the lost child by offering him sweets, balloons, and rides. This shows compassion and humanity.
concepts.real_example: People sometimes help lost children or strangers in difficult situations.

concepts.lesson: Earlier the child wanted many things, but after losing his parents he refuses everything and only asks for them. This shows how emotional needs are greater than material desires.
concepts.real_example: In difficult moments, people realize that relationships are more important than possessions.

concepts.lesson: The story teaches that love, care, and family relationships are more valuable than temporary pleasures like toys or sweets.
concepts.real_example: Family support is the most important source of happiness and security.
